name: Ubiquiti airMAX PrismStation Horn 90°
title: Ubiquiti airMAX PrismStation Horn 90°
short_description: 5GHz, 13 dBi, Beam Width 90°, pair with PrismStation AC or IsoStation
description: 5GHz, 13 dBi, Beam Width 90°, pair with PrismStation AC or IsoStation

detailed_description.heading: 5 GHz Beamwidth Horn antenna with 90° precise radiation angles for specific beam shaping.
detailed_description.text: The airMAX PrismStation Horn 5 90°, cataloged under the model manufacture part number Horn-5-90, is a pivotal innovation in Ubiquiti Networks' comprehensive portfolio of wireless network solutions, specifically engineered to augment the capabilities of the PrismStation series. This device is tailored for optimal performance in complex and densely populated radio frequency environments, delivering highly efficient and targeted wireless communication. Operating within the 5 GHz frequency spectrum, the Horn-5-90 is designed to provide expansive coverage with precise directivity, ensuring robust point-to-point (PtP) and point-to-multipoint (PtMP) connectivity.

detailed_description.heading: Compatibility
detailed_description.text: Compatible with airFiber, LTU and airMAX radios.

detailed_description.heading: Integration and Design
detailed_description.text: Integration with the PrismStation series is straightforward, thanks to the antenna's twist-lock mechanism, which ensures a secure and simple connection without the need for specialized tools or extensive installation procedures. This design not only facilitates rapid deployment and system scalability but also allows network operators to easily swap or upgrade antenna components as network requirements evolve. Moreover, the Horn-5-90 is also designed with compatibility in mind, seamlessly working with other Ubiquiti airMAX or LTU devices that support the same frequency band and connection interface. This interoperability allows for the creation of highly customized network deployments, catering to the specific coverage and performance needs of each installation.

detailed_description.heading: Precise Beam Shaping and RF Isolation
detailed_description.text: A defining feature of the Horn-5-90 is its 90° beamwidth antenna, which strikes a balance between broad coverage area and focused signal intensity. This wide beamwidth enables the device to cover larger areas or serve multiple clients within its range, making it exceptionally suitable for PtMP configurations where a single base station communicates with various client devices spread over a wide area. The antenna's design focuses the wireless signal in a specific direction, significantly reducing interference from or to adjacent networks, thereby enhancing the overall quality and reliability of the connection. Also, 60° and 90° versions have naturally attenuated side lobes and extremely low back radiation. They offer the best front-to-back ratio in the industry and the lowest side lobe radiation. Asymmetrical horn antennas are ideal for cluster sector installations with high co-location requirements. The Horn-5-90 boasts superior RF isolation, a characteristic inherent to its horn antenna design. This isolation is critical for minimizing noise and interference in crowded RF environments, ensuring that the device can maintain a clean and clear channel for communication. Such RF isolation is particularly valuable in urban areas, industrial sites, and other settings where the airwaves are congested with signals from a myriad of wireless devices.

detailed_description.heading: Durability and Construction
detailed_description.text: Durability is a hallmark of the Horn-5-90, built to endure the challenges of outdoor installation. The device is encased in a robust, weather-resistant shell that stands up to adverse conditions, including rain, wind, and extreme temperatures. This resilience is essential for ensuring the longevity and reliability of outdoor wireless networks, where continuous exposure to the elements can significantly impact performance.
